steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The equation \(4m = 3(n + p)\) has \(m\) multiplied by 4. To isolate \(m\), divide both sides by 4.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(m = \frac{3(n+p)}{4}\)': Exactly the result of dividing both sides by 4. ✓
  • Choice B — '\(m = 3(n+p)\)': Omits the division by 4; this would require the original equation to be \(m = 3(n+p)\). ✗
  • Choice C — '\(m = 3(n+p) - 4\)': Incorrectly subtracts 4 instead of dividing by 4. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(m = 3 - n - p - 4\)': Incorrectly distributes and subtracts rather than divides by 4. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
